objective: Repair `imobench/answerbench_v2.csv` so `imo-bench-algebra-036` has the reported short answer `$Y(x)=A+B/x-x$` in its own Short Answer column and again has exactly the six columns declared by the dataset header.
completion_criteria: Parse the CSV with a standards-compliant reader, assert that every row has six columns, assert the exact short-answer field for `imo-bench-algebra-036`, and pass any repository checks. The current main branch was independently reproduced as a five-column row.
non_results: Finite computation outside its stated range is not a proof of a universal claim.
A candidate is not a solution until independent and human review are recorded.
Restating a known reduction, checking examples, or giving an unproved key lemma is not terminal success.

baseline_review.requirement: Repeat when the authoritative status changes materially or the baseline is explicitly invalidated.

synthesis_summary: At the 2026-07-22 audit, upstream issue #13 remained open and current main was 96fa6c4cc3a9bb7450ee7b6773b659d3a030dace. The pinned 169,537-byte CSV has 400 data records: 399 width six and only algebra-036 width five under ordinary parsing; strict parsing rejects it. A temporary one-byte quote insertion at offset 13,618 yields a 169,538-byte file with SHA-256 0c05a0d4af9cbe3e70413b250d6c9cac1bfe4d848f6c196f83ed61ebef9ced16, 400 strict-parsed six-column records, and exact Short Answer $Y(x)=A+\frac{B}{x}-x$. This mandatory baseline epoch did not edit the released dataset or submit externally.

established_facts.claim: The pinned upstream CSV has exactly 400 data records under ordinary parsing, with width counts {5: 1, 6: 399}; the width-five record is algebra-036.
A quote inserted at byte offset 13,618 restores six fields on every record and exact target Short Answer $Y(x)=A+\frac{B}{x}-x$.
established_facts.evidence: Strict full-file parse, target assertion, and exact-diff checker in experiment 20260722-153113-23f885
established_facts.scope: The temporary one-byte candidate derived from the pinned baseline

established_facts.claim: The semantic-only checker is insufficient to exclude unrelated changes, while --reference excludes the tested unrelated mutation.
established_facts.scope: The checker and pinned baseline used in this epoch

strategies.family: CSV structural validation and minimal-patch certification
strategies.title: Authoritative GitHub status audit plus a hash-pinned CSV parse, temporary one-byte repair, and adversarial exact-diff checker controls
strategies.mechanism: A standards-library CSV parser exposes the unique malformed row; the issue-supplied answer token identifies the separator; a second checker certifies all row widths, exact target fields, and equality to the baseline plus one quote byte.
strategies.hypothesis: At upstream commit 96fa6c4cc3a9bb7450ee7b6773b659d3a030dace, algebra-036 is the unique five-field data record, strict CSV parsing rejects the malformed syntax, and inserting one quote before the answer separator restores all 400 records and the exact short-answer field without another change.
strategies.discriminating_test: Run python3 tools/baseline_answerbench_036.py on the pinned CSV and checker; require the baseline/repair signals and rejection of wrong-answer and unrelated-edit controls.
strategies.blocker: The mandatory baseline phase forbade applying the correction in this epoch; afterward, human authorization, CLA readiness, and upstream maintainer review remain necessary for external acceptance.
strategies.reopen_condition: Not applicable to the active route; refresh or redirect if upstream main, issue status, PR status, or target bytes change.

strategies.mechanism: Add a generic standards-library test that parses every maintained benchmark CSV and checks each data-row width against its header, with a target-literal regression assertion for issue #13.
strategies.hypothesis: A generic schema guard will fail on current main, pass on the one-byte repair, and catch future malformed rows without relying on problem-specific raw-byte patterns.
strategies.discriminating_test: Run the generic guard on the pinned baseline and temporary repair; require one uniquely identified failure before and zero failures after.
strategies.rationale: This is a preventive repository mechanism rather than a renamed one-byte patch, but it should remain secondary to the minimal correction unless maintainers request tests.
strategies.title: Add a generic standards-library test that parses every maintained benchmark CSV and checks each data-row width against its header, with a target-literal regression assertion for issue #13.

open_leads.description: Apply and verify the isolated one-byte patch against a freshly resolved upstream head.
open_leads.rationale: It is the cheapest path from verified baseline to the requested dataset correction.
open_leads.next_experiment: Run git ls-remote for upstream main, refresh if needed, then invoke the checker with --reference on the materialized candidate.

open_leads.description: Package the full-file checker as a regression test or PR verification command.
open_leads.rationale: The pinned repository tree has no existing CI/test harness for CSV widths, and semantic-only checks miss collateral edits.
open_leads.next_experiment: Run the checker on the fresh baseline and candidate from a clean checkout and record the exact output.

open_leads.description: Seek upstream acceptance after owner approval.
open_leads.rationale: Maintainer review is the only external acceptance path identified by CONTRIBUTING.md.
open_leads.next_experiment: Prepare a one-byte PR diff and validation transcript linked to issue #13; do not publish without human authorization.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Use semantic-only CSV validation as the complete contribution certificate
ruled_out.scope: Proposed algebra-036 repair candidates against the pinned baseline
ruled_out.reason: It accepts a candidate containing an unrelated problem-ID mutation.
ruled_out.evidence: The unrelated_edit control has semantic_only_returncode 0 and with_reference_returncode 1.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A new checker proves full-file equality outside the target edit by another exact mechanism.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Solve or re-derive the functional equation before repairing the CSV
ruled_out.reason: Issue #13 and the existing raw token supply the exact stored answer literal; the failure is CSV quoting, not mathematical uncertainty.
ruled_out.evidence: Issue #13 body, pinned raw bytes, and successful structural repair
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The authoritative source retracts or disputes the stored answer literal.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat issue #13 as already repaired upstream
ruled_out.reason: The issue was open, main retained the malformed bytes, and both repository PR searches returned zero.
ruled_out.evidence: Pinned API snapshots and baseline experiment
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A newer upstream commit, issue transition, or matching pull request appears.

next_session.objective: Materialize and certify the exact one-byte dataset correction if the authoritative baseline is unchanged.
next_session.stop_condition: Stop or redirect if issue #13 is closed, a competing PR exists, upstream bytes changed, the checker fails, or the candidate diff is not exactly one inserted quote.

tactical_memory.current_bottleneck: The technical edit is isolated; the remaining bottlenecks are refreshing the upstream base, applying the patch in a technical epoch, human approval, CLA readiness, and maintainer review.

failure_signatures.signature: An unclosed quoted Problem field can be tolerated as a five-field record by ordinary parsing but rejected as malformed by strict parsing; semantic-only repair checks can miss unrelated edits.

reusable_assets.name: check_answerbench_036.py
reusable_assets.use: Verify six-column schema, target fields, and one-byte-only diff

reusable_assets.name: baseline_answerbench_036.py and result
reusable_assets.use: Reproduce the defect and adversarially exercise the checker

constraints_learned.constraint: Always use the checker with --reference for the contribution gate.
constraints_learned.scope: Any proposed repair derived from the pinned baseline
constraints_learned.evidence: The unrelated-edit control passes semantic-only checks and fails the exact-diff guard.

constraints_learned.constraint: Preserve the repository's exact LaTeX literal $Y(x)=A+\frac{B}{x}-x$ rather than normalizing it.
constraints_learned.evidence: Issue #13 body, pinned source bytes, and the wrong-answer control

constraints_learned.constraint: Refresh the upstream baseline before editing if main no longer equals 96fa6c4cc3a9bb7450ee7b6773b659d3a030dace.
constraints_learned.evidence: The exact-diff certificate is hash-bound to the pinned source.

decision_history.prediction: Ordinary parsing would show 399 six-field and one five-field row; a one-byte candidate would yield 400 six-field rows and pass all controls.
decision_history.observation: The width prediction and all candidate controls passed; strict parsing of the malformed baseline raised a CSV syntax error while strict parsing of the repair passed.
decision_history.surprise: The initial strict-parser run failed earlier than the predicted width count, providing a stronger syntax-level diagnostic; the harness was revised to preserve both tolerant-width and strict-syntax signals.
decision_history.next_discriminator: Resolve upstream main; if unchanged, materialize the one-byte candidate and run the narrow checker with the pinned source as --reference.

reduction_ledger.ambient_space: All single-byte insertions into the pinned 169,537-byte CSV: 169,538 positions times 256 byte values
reduction_ledger.eliminated_or_quotiented: 43,401,727 candidates eliminated by the unique answer token, preceding comma, required CSV closing quote, and issue-specified target
reduction_ledger.represented_space_after: 1 candidate edit script: insert byte 0x22 at zero-based offset 13,618
reduction_ledger.coverage_scope: Single-byte insertion repairs of the pinned upstream CSV only
reduction_ledger.soundness_basis: Unique answer-token count, local separator inspection, strict parse of all repaired records, exact target-field assertion, and exact candidate/reference byte equality
reduction_ledger.remaining_unknown: Arbitrary multi-byte rewrites were not classified, upstream may move, and maintainer acceptance is external.
reduction_ledger.next_bulk_elimination: None; no further search is justified. Refresh upstream and apply the already isolated edit.

prior_art_decisions.exact_delta: Issue #13 reports the merged fields. This epoch adds a hash-bound full-file reproduction, identifies the exact one-byte edit, supplies an exact-diff checker, and tests strict-parser, wrong-answer, and unrelated-edit controls.
prior_art_decisions.duplicate_risk: A fix could appear after the timestamp, exist in an unindexed fork, or be submitted under wording absent from the two repository PR searches.
prior_art_decisions.comparison_test: Before any patch or PR, resolve current main and rerun repository issue/PR searches for answerbench_v2 and imo-bench-algebra-036.
